item.description: In this webinar, our experts will share insights from the front lines of incident response.

item.title: Learn Hub | Bridewell
item.description: Boost your cyber security knowledge with expert insights and easy-to-understand resources on our Learn Hub.

item.title: Intelligence Insights: Feb 2026
item.description: This month’s Threat Insights leverages data from Bridewell’s global MDR and Incident Response engagements to expose the top malicious infrastructure of February 2026.

item.title: Addressing AI Hallucinations in Security Operations: A Practical Framework
item.description: The concern about AI hallucinations is legitimate. When a large language model generates plausible-sounding but factually incorrect information, the consequences in security operations can be serious. A hallucinated indicator of compromise could trigger unnecessary incident response. A fabricated remediation step could make things worse. For CISOs evaluating AI adoption, understanding both the risk and the mitigation strategies is essential.

item.title: Reddit Fined £14.5m Over Children's Privacy Failures
item.description: Reddit Fined £14.5m Over Children's Privacy Failures

item.title: Why Point in Time Cloud Posture Assessments Are No Longer Enough: The Case for Continuous CSPM
item.description: Cloud environments are inherently dynamic. Resources are deployed, modified, and removed daily, configurations are updated and access permissions change frequently. This is often driven by automation, with limited visibility or oversight from security teams. Each change introduces the potential for misconfiguration, unintentionally creating exposure and attack paths that can be exploited if left undetected or poorly prioritised.

item.title: AI in Security Operations: Separating Capability from Hype
item.description: The security operations challenge is well documented. True positive rates often sit below five percent. Alert fatigue is endemic. Skilled analysts are scarce and expensive. These pressures have driven significant interest in AI in security operations, but the conversation has become clouded by vendor marketing and inflated expectations. For SOC managers responsible for delivering measurable outcomes, understanding where AI actually delivers value is essential.

item.title: The Booking.com Phishing Campaign Targeting Hotels and Customers
item.description: Since the start of January, we have observed a resurgence in malicious activity targeting the hotel and retail sector. Discover more as we uncover the phishing campaign.

item.title: What is an Agentic SOC? Understanding the Evolution Beyond Automation
item.description: The AI hype cycle has created genuine confusion in security operations. Vendors promise autonomous SOCs that will revolutionise threat detection and response, but for organisations operating critical national infrastructure, the distinction between marketing claims and operational reality matters. When uptime is non-negotiable and regulatory scrutiny is intense, understanding what an agentic SOC actually delivers becomes essential.

item.title: Intelligence Insights: Jan 2026
item.description: This month’s Threat Insights leverages data from Bridewell’s global MDR and Incident Response engagements to expose the top malicious infrastructure of January 2025.

item.title: Government Cyber Security Policy: Cyber Incident Exercising – What You Need to Know
item.description: Cyber threats continue to pose significant risks to government organisations, highlighting the importance of effective preparedness to mitigate potential impacts. To enhance resilience throughout the public sector, the UK Government has implemented a requirement for Cyber Incident Exercising as part of the Government Cyber Security Policy. This blog summarises the policy’s scope, key requirements, and steps government organisations should take to prepare.

item.title: How To Stay Ahead of Evolving Cyber and Data Privacy Regulation and Legislation
item.description: New cyber and data privacy regulations and legislations continue to be announced and written into law in the UK and abroad. Whilst technology continues to advance, providing new capabilities and benefits, governments must respond to address matters including cyber threats, resilience, usage and ethics.

item.title: Secure by Design, Delivered: A Practitioner’s Playbook for Government Teams | Bridewell
item.description: This blog distils Secure by Design down into actionable building blocks and provides guidance on how project teams can embed Secure by Design requirements into agile project delivery.

item.title: UK Public Cyber Crime Incidents Rise 37% Over Five Years
item.description: UK Public Cyber Crime Incidents Rise 37% Over Five Years

item.title: Why Point in Time Cloud Posture Assessments Are No Longer Enough: The Case for Continuous CSPM
item.description: Cloud environments are inherently dynamic. Resources are deployed, modified, and removed daily, configurations are updated and access permissions change frequently. This is often driven by automation, with limited visibility or oversight from security teams. Each change introduces the potential for misconfiguration, unintentionally creating exposure and attack paths that can be exploited if left undetected or poorly prioritised.
